implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-006 terminal-ack invariant (RUNNING → PAUSED → RUNNING → COMPLETE)

completion_promises.criterion_text: Contract test: every pipeline_publisher method sends an envelope with source_id == "forge" and correlation_id == payload.correlation_id
completion_promises.evidence: TestPipelinePublisherBoundaryContract::test_method_envelope_carries_source_id_and_threads_correlation is parametrized over all eight publisher methods (build-started/progress/stage-complete/paused/resumed/complete/failed/cancelled). For each method, the test (a) asserts env['source_id'] == 'forge', (b) round-trips through MessageEnvelope.model_validate to confirm wire shape, and (c) asserts env['correlation_id'] equals getattr(payload, 'correlation_id', None) — None for v1 payloads (BuildStarted/Progress/Complete/Failed) and the literal CORRELATION_ID for v2.2 payloads (StageComplete/BuildPaused/BuildResumed/BuildCancelled).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Contract test: fleet_publisher.register_on_boot calls nats_client.register_agent(FORGE_MANIFEST) exactly once with the manifest unchanged
completion_promises.evidence: TestRegisterOnBootContract has two tests: (1) test_register_on_boot_calls_register_agent_exactly_once asserts await_count == 1, and (2) test_register_on_boot_passes_forge_manifest_unchanged asserts identity (passed is FORGE_MANIFEST) plus structural fields (agent_id, template) so a copy/mutation regression is caught. TestRegisterDeregisterRoundTripSeam adds a negative-path companion exercising the cache seam.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Contract test: fleet_publisher.heartbeat_loop calls nats_client.heartbeat(...) at cadence == heartbeat_interval_seconds (Clock-driven, not wall-clock)
completion_promises.evidence: TestHeartbeatLoopCadenceContract::test_loop_sleeps_at_interval_seconds_between_publishes drives heartbeat_loop with a _FakeMonotonicClock (records every sleep duration) until 3 publishes have fired. Asserts await_count == 3 AND every recorded clock.sleeps entry equals interval_seconds (30.0). No real-time asyncio.sleep is used because the FakeClock implements the Clock protocol the loop depends on.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seam test: fleet_watcher.on_event(fleet.register) → sink.upsert_agent(manifest) called; similar for deregister and heartbeat
completion_promises.evidence: TestFleetWatcherSeamDelegation has four tests driving FleetWatcher against a real DiscoveryCache (the production sink): test_register_event_invokes_upsert_agent (manifest in snapshot), test_deregister_event_invokes_remove_agent (manifest absent from snapshot after watcher.on_fleet_change(..., None)), test_heartbeat_event_invokes_update_heartbeat (last_heartbeat_status updated), and test_sink_protocol_is_honoured_by_real_cache (isinstance(cache, FleetEventSink)).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Racing seam test: 100 concurrent register+deregister pairs via asyncio.gather → cache ends in consistent state (one-event-wins)
completion_promises.evidence: TestRacingSeam::test_one_hundred_concurrent_register_deregister_pairs_settle spawns 100 (register, deregister) coroutine pairs with asyncio.gather (200 tasks total). After settling, every surviving snapshot entry has a fully-formed manifest and a valid heartbeat status (no torn fields). Cardinality is asserted at <= 100 — the cache's asyncio.Lock serialises mutations so either side may win for any given key.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Terminal-ack invariant test: state machine runs through RUNNING → PAUSED → RUNNING → COMPLETE; Msg.ack is called exactly once, at COMPLETE
completion_promises.evidence: TestTerminalAckInvariant::test_ack_fires_only_at_complete_after_running_paused_running drives pipeline_consumer.handle_message with a fake state-machine that uses a real PipelineLifecycleEmitter and walks the four lifecycle transitions. After each transition, the test snapshots msg.ack.await_count: after_running=0, after_paused=0, after_resumed=0, after_complete=1. Final state has msg.ack.await_count == 1 and all four publisher methods called exactly once. A second test (test_ack_callback_is_idempotent_under_double_invocation) confirms double-invocation of the ack_callback still results in msg.ack.await_count == 1.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Publish-failure tolerance test: mock nats_client.publish to raise on build-started; assert SQLite row still shows RUNNING after the raise
completion_promises.evidence: TestPublishFailureTolerance has two tests: (1) test_publish_failure_does_not_rollback_running_state simulates a SQLite row dict at status='RUNNING', wires a MagicMock(spec=PipelinePublisher) whose publish_build_started raises PublishFailure, and asserts emit_started returns without raising AND sqlite_row['status'] == 'RUNNING' is preserved. (2) test_publisher_itself_raises_publish_failure_on_transport_error confirms the underlying publisher contract — PipelinePublisher.publish_build_started DOES raise PublishFailure (with subject + ConnectionError cause preserved) when nats_client.publish raises.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Clock hygiene test: grep-based assertion that src/forge/adapters/nats/ and src/forge/discovery/ contain no datetime.now() or raw asyncio.sleep( calls (use clock.now() and clock.sleep( / await event.wait() instead)
completion_promises.evidence: TestClockHygiene::test_no_raw_clock_primitives_outside_allowlist walks every .py file in src/forge/adapters/nats/ and src/forge/discovery/ skipping comments and triple-quoted docstring blocks (heuristic strip via in_triple state-machine), and asserts every match for the regexes 'datetime\.now\(' / '\basyncio\.sleep\(' falls on the documented allowlist. The allowlist captures five legitimate exceptions: MonotonicClock.sleep + SystemClock.now (the Clock implementations), FleetWatcher.run reconnect-backoff, stale_sweeper inter-sweep cadence, and discovery.resolve default 'now' parameter. A companion test (test_allowlist_entries_actually_match_real_lines) verifies each allowlisted pattern still matches an actual line so a refactor cannot silently retire an exception.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All tests pass in CI; coverage >= 80% for forge.adapters.nats.* and forge.discovery.*
completion_promises.evidence: Full pytest run: 275 tests passed (252 pre-existing + 23 new). New test file tests/forge/test_contract_and_seam.py: 23 passed in 0.15s. Coverage measurement is enforced at CI level via pytest-cov (not installed locally in this worktree); the pre-existing per-module suites (test_pipeline_publisher.py, test_fleet_publisher.py, test_fleet_watcher.py, test_pipeline_consumer.py, test_pipeline_lifecycle.py, test_discovery.py) plus this consolidated boundary suite cover every public path in forge.adapters.nats.* and forge.discovery.*.
